basics.name: G Vibu Vignesh
basics.label: Systems Engineer (Rust)
basics.summary: Systems Engineer specializing in Rust and low-level Linux kernel primitives. Experienced in building high-performance cloud-native infrastructure, including OCI-compliant container runtimes, CRI protocols, and custom process isolation layers (cgroups v2, namespaces). Combines deep systems programming expertise with a strong background in deploying optimized AI workloads, containerization, and contributing to core open-source OCI tooling.

location.address: 43/1, Street no. 7, Sri Senthur Happy Homes, Kovai Road, Annur

work.name: Tinkerblox Inc
work.summary: Developed oci low level container runtime to deploy applications in linux namespaces that is a drop-in replacement for runc and also worked on cri-protocol grpc that can communicate with kubernetes and deploy applications in cloud infrastructure
highlights: Engineered a high-performance, OCI-compliant container runtime in Rust as a drop-in replacement for runc, significantly accelerating container startup and execution times.
Architected a gRPC-based Container Runtime Interface (CRI) protocol to enable seamless communication with Kubernetes and orchestrate workloads across cloud infrastructure.
Leveraged Rust’s memory safety to implement low-level Linux primitives (clone, pivot_root, unshare) via nix and libc crates, reducing system call latency without garbage collection overhead.
Enforced strict process isolation and predictable resource allocation by implementing Linux Namespaces and Cgroups v2.
Ensured full OCI specification parity to guarantee seamless compatibility and integration with existing container engines and orchestration workflows.

work.name: DoaZ (Remote)
work.summary: Developed and deployed a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) chatbot to provide rapid answers from complex Korean Construction Rules & Regulation documents.
highlights: Improved information retrieval accuracy from 60% to 80% by implementing Contextual Chunking for document preprocessing.
Utilized DSPy for advanced prompt optimization, enhancing chatbot response quality and relevance.
Deployed open-source LLMs (Qwen 148 & 32B) on Vastai GPU servers using VLLM for efficient inference.
Built and maintained FastAPI endpoints for seamless interaction between the AI backend and the frontend application.
Deployed LLMs using candle-rust library for faster LLM inference using Rust Programming Language
Deployed web-applications using Docker to AWS

work.name: Upwork (Remote)
work.summary: Built a rag chatbot that searched through pdf files and csv files to answer user questions
highlights: Engineered a PDF chatbot featuring a unique user-driven document selection architecture prior to Q&A.
Developed a CSV-based chatbot leveraging Langchain's Pandas agent framework for data interaction and analysis.
Created a web-search-enabled chatbot integrating DuckDuckGo to answer queries with real-time information.
Enhanced overall chatbot functionality using Python and various OpenAI models.

work.name: Gilbert Research Institute (Coimbatore)
work.summary: Learnt ML concepts and built Lung and Liver Tumor Detection ML models
highlights: Developed a medical image analysis solution for Lung and Liver Tumor segmentation.
Utilized PyTorch and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to build and train the segmentation models.
Communicated effectively with faculty and staff, incorporating feedback for improvement.
Collaborated within teams to analyze problems and develop data-driven solutions.

opensource.description: Contributed a fix to GPT-Researcher's RAG pipeline, enabling functional metadata filtering by correcting parameter propagation between the research agent and vector store wrappers
Added support for the new `SECCOMP_FILTER_FLAG_WAIT_KILLABLE_RECV` flag in youki to align with OCI runtime spec v1.3 updates.
Added `SECCOMP_FILTER_FLAG_WAIT_KILLABLE_RECV` to the seccomp configuration to support OCI Runtime Spec v1.3.0 and improve killable wait behavior in seccomp user notifications.
